title: Wire score.get_mixability through CacheStore (L2)
description: Layer the mixability cache the same way. L1 = score._mixability_cache (per CLAUDE.md: keyed by content.ID, now cached). L2 = CacheStore.mixability.
acceptanceCriteria: score.get_mixability(content, db) returns identical {overall, components} dict to v0 on miss; same on warm hit.
When /api/apply edits cues for a content_id, that content's mixability row is invalidated (cues affect intro/outro bars - per PRD §5 Flow E).
tests/test_score.py adds L2 hit / miss-and-populate cases plus a test that invalidate_track(content_id) clears the mixability row but preserves energy_curve / classification / similarity_vector.

steps.details: L1 -> L2 -> compute. Score is a single float; components is a dict.

Per PRD §5 Flow E, /api/apply must invalidate ONLY mixability for the affected content_id - NOT energy/classification/similarity. Add CacheStore.invalidate_mixability(content_id) (in addition to invalidate_track(content_id) which clears everything). Call it from the /api/apply writer hook (TASK-026 adds the snapshot invalidation; this task adds the mixability-only call).
tests/test_score.py: warm hit short-circuit; after apply, mixability cleared but other tables intact (assert energy_curve still has the row).

technicalNotes: Why mixability is the only one cue-dependent: intro/outro bar counts come from cue positions; energy/classification/similarity are purely ANLZ-derived.
